schools.summary: Gill: this was the second of the seven lots, Benjamin's being the first.
Henry: Simeon's lot was drawn after Judah's, within Judah's inheritance.

themes: the second lot came forth to Simeon... their inheritance was within the inheritance of the children of Judah
Jacob's prophecy of scattering fulfilled, yet mercy in the discipline
I will divide them in Jacob, and scatter them in Israel (Gen. 49:7)

schools.summary: Gill: Beersheba and Sheba, the particle sometimes used so, lest the count exceed the thirteen cities given; Moladah among them.

themes: they had in their inheritance Beersheba, and Sheba, and Moladah
Beersheba, the patriarchs' well of the oath, in the heritage
he hath remembered his covenant for ever... which he made with Abraham (Ps. 105:8-9)

schools.summary: Gill: Hazar-shual, of which he speaks elsewhere.
Keil & Delitzsch: Hazar-shual, Balah, and Azem among the cities.

themes: And Hazarshual, and Balah, and Azem
God giving a portion even in the dry and wild places
he... satisfied the longing soul (Ps. 107:4-9)

schools.summary: Gill: these were all cities of Judah; Eltolad the same with Tolad, Bethul with Bethuel; Hormah named with them.

themes: And Eltolad, and Bethul, and Hormah
Hormah, scene of both presumptuous defeat and faithful victory
without me ye can do nothing (John 15:5)

schools.summary: Gill: Ziklag, another of the cities of Judah.
Clarke: Beth-marcaboth, 'the house of chariots,' likely where war-chariots were kept.

themes: And Ziklag, and Bethmarcaboth, and Hazarsusah
Ziklag, David's refuge where he encouraged himself in the LORD
David encouraged himself in the LORD his God (1 Sam. 30:6)

schools.summary: Gill: Beth-lebaoth, perhaps the same with Lebaoth; some say an idol-temple to lions was here; thirteen cities with their villages.

themes: And Bethlebaoth, and Sharuhen; thirteen cities and their villages
God turning even the dens of idolatry into his people's dwellings
they shall not hurt nor destroy in all my holy mountain (Isa. 11:6-9)

schools.summary: Gill: Ain and Remmon were cities of Judah; four cities with their villages.

themes: Ain, Remmon, and Ether, and Ashan; four cities and their villages
God providing refreshment and fruit even in the dry south
I will make the wilderness a pool of water (Isa. 41:18)

schools.summary: Gill: not only the suburbs but the scattered towns and villages round about.
Keil & Delitzsch: the close of Simeon's inheritance.

themes: all the villages... round about these cities to Baalathbeer... This is the inheritance of... Simeon
God leaving none of his people without a place
he gathereth together the outcasts of Israel (Ps. 147:2)

schools.summary: Gill: this is why no border of Simeon is described, it being within Judah's, already given.
Keil & Delitzsch: Simeon's inheritance taken from Judah's too-large portion.

themes: the part of the children of Judah was too much for them: therefore the children of Simeon had their inheritance within
the greater tribe sharing its abundance with the lesser
your abundance may be a supply for their want... that there may be equality (2 Cor. 8:14)

schools.summary: Gill: introduces the third lot for Zebulun.
Keil & Delitzsch: Sarid as the centre of the southern boundary.

themes: the third lot came up for the children of Zebulun... the border of their inheritance was unto Sarid
God remembering all his people, each in due order
he shall choose our inheritance for us (Ps. 47:4)

schools.summary: Gill: the border went westward toward the Mediterranean, fulfilling the prophecies of Jacob and Moses that Zebulun should dwell by the sea and be a haven of ships.

themes: their border went up toward the sea... to the river that is before Jokneam
Zebulun set by the sea, fulfilling Jacob's dying prophecy
Zebulun shall dwell at the haven of the sea (Gen. 49:13); not one word hath failed (1 Kgs 8:56)

schools.summary: Gill: this describes the southern border, going from west to east.
Keil & Delitzsch: the border eastward to Chisloth-tabor and Daberath.

themes: turned from Sarid eastward... unto the border of Chislothtabor... to Daberath, and... to Japhia
the inheritance marked with exactness from sea to mountain
thou hast set all the borders of the earth (Ps. 74:17)

schools.summary: Gill: Gittah-hepher was the native place of Jonah the prophet, where Jerom says his grave was shown.

themes: from thence passeth on along on the east to Gittahhepher
Gath-hepher, the birthplace of Jonah, whose sign points to Christ
as Jonas was three days... so shall the Son of man be (Matt. 12:40)

schools.summary: Gill: this is the northern border, taking a circuit; of these places little is known but that Jerom places them in Zebulun; the outgoings in the valley of Jiphthah-el.

themes: the border compasseth it on the north side to Hannathon... in the valley of Jiphthahel
the very name 'God opens' breathing of his working
I have set before thee an open door, and no man can shut it (Rev. 3:8)

schools.summary: Gill: of the first cities we read nowhere else.
Keil & Delitzsch: Kattath, Nahallal, Shimron, Idalah, and Bethlehem, twelve cities.

themes: Kattath, and Nahallal, and Shimron, and Idalah, and Bethlehem: twelve cities
a Bethlehem, 'house of bread,' pointing to the Bread of Life
I am the living bread which came down from heaven (John 6:51)

schools.summary: Gill: allotted and divided according to the number of its families.
Keil & Delitzsch: the close of Zebulun's inheritance.

themes: This is the inheritance of the children of Zebulun according to their families, these cities with their villages
the LORD finishing what he begins for his people
he which hath begun a good work in you will perform it (Phil. 1:6)

schools.summary: Gill: this was the fourth of the seven lots drawn at Shiloh.
Henry: Issachar a numerous tribe, its lot from Jordan to the great sea.

themes: the fourth lot came out to Issachar, for the children of Issachar according to their families
Issachar, the strong, laborious, discerning tribe
the children of Issachar... had understanding of the times (1 Chr. 12:32)

schools.summary: Gill: Jezreel was a royal seat in the time of Ahab, near Maximianopolis, a large village in the great plain; Chesulloth and Shunem were near.

themes: their border was toward Jezreel, and Chesulloth, and Shunem
Jezreel, the great plain of Israel's battles and God's judgments
I will plead with them there for my people (Joel 3:2)

schools.summary: Gill: Hapharaim is by Jerom called Aphraim, a city of Issachar, near a village Affarea.
Keil & Delitzsch: Hapharaim, Shion, and Anaharath.

themes: And Hapharaim, and Shion, and Anaharath
the enduring cities a token of God's lasting gift
the gifts and calling of God are without repentance (Rom. 11:29)

schools.summary: Gill: the first Jerom calls Rabboth in the tribe of Issachar.
Keil & Delitzsch: Rabbith, Kishion, and Abez.

themes: And Rabbith, and Kishion, and Abez
Kishion, later a city of the Levites, set among Issachar
they shall teach Jacob thy judgments, and Israel thy law (Deut. 33:10)

schools.summary: Gill: Remeth seems the same with Jarmuth and with Ramoth.
Keil & Delitzsch: Remeth stands as Jarmuth in the list of Levitical towns (Joshua 21:29).

themes: And Remeth, and Engannim, and Enhaddah, and Bethpazzez
the fountain-cities a figure of the living water God gives
with thee is the fountain of life (Ps. 36:9)

schools.summary: Gill: Tabor, a mountain generally supposed, though not certainly, to be that of our Lord's transfiguration; the border went out at Jordan, sixteen cities.

themes: the coast reacheth to Tabor... and the outgoings of their border were at Jordan: sixteen cities
Tabor, by tradition the mount of Christ's transfiguration
his face did shine as the sun (Matt. 17:2)

schools.summary: Gill: this fell to them by lot, as before described.
Keil & Delitzsch: the close of Issachar's inheritance.

themes: This is the inheritance of the tribe of the children of Issachar according to their families
the LORD providing entirely, giving the diligent their reward
God is not unrighteous to forget your work and labour of love (Heb. 6:10)

schools.summary: Gill: this entitled them to the inheritance next described.
Henry: Asher's lot lay upon the coast of the great sea; of this tribe was Anna the prophetess.

themes: And the fifth lot came out for the tribe of the children of Asher according to their families
Asher, 'the blessed,' the tribe of Anna who saw the Christ
Anna, a prophetess... of the tribe of Aser... spake of him to all (Luke 2:36-38)

schools.summary: Gill: Helkath seems the same with Hukok, lying above Ptolemais.
Keil & Delitzsch: Helkath, Hali, Beten, and Achshaph.

themes: And their border was Helkath, and Hali, and Beten, and Achshaph
the conquered king's city become a tribe's and a Levite's home
the kingdom... given to the people of the saints of the most High (Dan. 7:27)

schools.summary: Gill: of Alammelech and Amad there is no mention elsewhere.
Keil & Delitzsch: the border reaching to Carmel westward and Shihor-libnath.

themes: And Alammelech, and Amad, and Misheal; and reacheth to Carmel westward
Carmel, the mount of Elijah's triumph over Baal, on Asher's border
the LORD, he is the God; the LORD, he is the God (1 Kgs 18:39)

schools.summary: The Danites going up to take Leshem when their coast was too little, and naming it Dan: 'and the coast of the children of Dan went out too little for them: therefore the children of Dan went up to fight against Leshem, and took it, and smote it with the edge of the sword, and possessed it, and dwelt therein, and called Leshem, Dan, after the name of Dan their father' — the failure and the migration — 'and the coast of the children of Dan went out too little for them: therefore the children of Dan went up to fight against Leshem, and took it... and called Leshem, Dan, after the name of Dan their father': Dan, finding their coast too narrow, went north and took Leshem, calling it Dan (the witnesses noting the significance — the failure of Dan to possess the portion God had given them, hemmed in by the Amorites and Philistines they had not driven out, and their seeking of a portion elsewhere rather than possessing their own by faith, the coast not truly too small, but too little held because the foe was not driven out, the Danites turning north for an easier conquest, 'the Amorites forced the children of Dan into the mountain... they would not suffer them to come down to the valley,' Judges 1:34, the lesson — that the people of God often forfeit their proper portion by unbelief and sloth, and seek elsewhere what they fail to claim at home, the inheritance lost not for want of God's gift but for want of faith to possess it, the truth that what God gives must be possessed by faith and courage, the failure to drive out the foe leaving the portion unenjoyed, 'ye have not, because ye ask not... ye ask amiss,' James 4:2-3, the figure — the believer who shrinks from the appointed conflict and the promised portion, settling for a lesser inheritance won more easily, the Danite migration a step toward the idolatry that would later mark Dan, 'the children of Dan set up the graven image,' Judges 18:30, the truth that the easier path chosen in unbelief leads away from God's appointment, the figure of the inheritance forfeited by unbelief and the portion sought elsewhere, the call to possess the appointed portion by faith rather than seek an easier, the warning that the path of least resistance may lead to apostasy, the figure of the failure to possess the assigned inheritance and the seeking of a portion elsewhere); Dan, their coast too narrow because the foe was not driven out, going north to take Leshem, the easier conquest sought (the failure to possess, Judges 1:34) — the migration (against Leshem); 'and smote it with the edge of the sword, and possessed it, and dwelt therein, and called Leshem, Dan, after the name of Dan their father' (Dan possessing Leshem and naming it after their father, the portion sought elsewhere rather than the appointed land claimed by faith, a step toward the idolatry that would mark Dan, the inheritance forfeited by unbelief, James 4:2-3; Judges 18:30) — the figure (the believer who settles for a lesser inheritance won more easily, the providence, the migration, Joshua's own inheritance to follow, vv.49) — Calvin, Keil, Ellicott, Gill.

debates.question: What does Dan's migration to take Leshem signify?

sides.position: The failure to possess the assigned inheritance and the seeking of a portion elsewhere - Dan's coast too little held because the foe was not driven out (Judges 1:34), the people of God forfeiting their proper portion by unbelief and sloth rather than possessing it by faith (James 4:2-3), the easier path chosen in unbelief a step toward the idolatry that would later mark Dan (Judges 18:30)

themes: And the coast of the children of Dan went out too little for them: therefore the children of Dan went up to fight against Leshem, and took it, and smote it with the edge of the sword, and possessed it, and dwelt therein, and called Leshem, Dan, after the name of Dan their father
the Danites going up to take Leshem when their coast was too little (the crux of the failure to possess the assigned inheritance and the seeking of a portion elsewhere)
the failure of Dan to possess the portion God had given, hemmed in by the foe they had not driven out, and their seeking of a portion elsewhere rather than possessing their own by faith
the people of God forfeiting their proper portion by unbelief, the easier path a step toward the idolatry that would later mark Dan (Judges 1:34; James 4:2-3; Judges 18:30)

schools.summary: The close of the inheritance of Dan: 'this is the inheritance of the tribe of the children of Dan according to their families, these cities with their villages' — the verse closes the portion of Dan, the cities and villages of the tribe (the this is the inheritance of the tribe of the children of Dan according to their families, the inheritance of Dan, the these cities with their villages, the cities and villages, the figure, the inheritance of Dan set down, the portion of God's appointment, the truth that the cities of Dan are recorded as the memorial of the inheritance, the figure of the inheritance possessed and recorded, the portion of God's people, the inheritance of Dan, the portion set down, vv.40); the inheritance of Dan by families (the portion) — the inheritance (of Dan); 'these cities with their villages' (the cities and villages of Dan, the portion, the figure, the inheritance possessed and recorded, the providence, the inheritance, the close of the division and Joshua's portion to follow, vv.49) — Keil and Delitzsch, Gill.

themes: This is the inheritance of the tribe of the children of Dan according to their families, these cities with their villages
the close of the inheritance of Dan
the verse closing the portion of Dan, the cities and villages of the tribe
the inheritance of God's appointment set down

schools.summary: That when the land was divided, Israel gave Joshua his inheritance last of all: 'when they had made an end of dividing the land for inheritance by their coasts, the children of Israel gave an inheritance to Joshua the son of Nun among them' — the leader rewarded last — 'when they had made an end of dividing the land for inheritance by their coasts, the children of Israel gave an inheritance to Joshua the son of Nun among them': only when all the tribes were settled did Joshua receive his own portion (the witnesses noting the significance — the self-denial and equity of Joshua, who, though leader and conqueror, took no portion until every tribe was provided, putting the people before himself, Joshua, who might have claimed the first and best, waiting until last, the servant who seeks not his own but the people's good, the lesson — that the true leader serves the people first and himself last, the greatest among them the servant of all, 'whosoever will be chief among you, let him be your servant,' Matthew 20:27, the truth that Joshua's humility and self-denial were a pattern, taking his portion among the people and not above them, 'look not every man on his own things, but every man also on the things of others,' Philippians 2:4, the figure — the leader who, like Christ, came not to be ministered unto but to minister, putting himself last that the people might be served, 'the Son of man came... to minister, and to give his life a ransom for many,' Mark 10:45, the truth that the reward of the faithful servant comes last but is sure, the inheritance not forgotten, the figure of the self-denying leader who serves first and is rewarded last, the call to seek the good of others before our own, the assurance that the faithful servant is not forgotten in the giving, the figure of the leader who serves first and is rewarded last, the self-denial of the faithful servant); the whole land divided among the tribes before Joshua's portion was given, the leader waiting until all were settled (the order of the giving, Matthew 20:27) — the completion (the land divided); 'the children of Israel gave an inheritance to Joshua the son of Nun among them' (Joshua receiving his inheritance last and among the people, the self-denial of the leader who put the people first, a pattern of Christ who came to minister, the servant of all, Philippians 2:4; Mark 10:45) — the figure (the leader who came not to be ministered unto but to minister, the providence, the portion, Timnathserah given by the word of the LORD to follow, vv.50) — Calvin, Keil, Ellicott, Gill.

debates.question: What does Joshua receiving his inheritance last signify?

sides.position: The leader who serves first and is rewarded last, the self-denial of the faithful servant - Joshua, though leader and conqueror, taking no portion until every tribe was provided, putting the people before himself, the true leader serving the people first and himself last (Matthew 20:27; Philippians 2:4), a figure of Christ who came not to be ministered unto but to minister (Mark 10:45)

themes: When they had made an end of dividing the land for inheritance by their coasts, the children of Israel gave an inheritance to Joshua the son of Nun among them
that Israel gave Joshua his inheritance last of all (the crux of the leader who serves first and is rewarded last)
the self-denial and equity of Joshua, who, though leader and conqueror, took no portion until every tribe was provided, putting the people before himself
the true leader serving the people first and himself last, a figure of Christ who came not to be ministered unto but to minister (Matthew 20:27; Philippians 2:4; Mark 10:45)

schools.summary: That Joshua received Timnathserah, the city he asked, according to the word of the LORD, and built and dwelt in it: 'according to the word of the LORD they gave him the city which he asked, even Timnathserah in mount Ephraim: and he built the city, and dwelt therein' — the modest portion by God's word — 'according to the word of the LORD they gave him the city which he asked, even Timnathserah in mount Ephraim: and he built the city, and dwelt therein': Joshua asked and received a single city, Timnathserah, by the word of the LORD, and built it up to dwell in (the witnesses noting the significance — the modesty of the great leader, who asked not for a wide portion but for one city, and that given by the word of the LORD and built up by his own hand, the conqueror of Canaan content with a small inheritance which he must yet labour to make habitable, Joshua asking little, receiving by God's word, and building it himself, the humility of the faithful servant in his reward, the lesson — that the faithful servant is content with a modest portion, seeking God's appointment rather than his own aggrandisement, and labours to improve what he is given, 'having food and raiment let us be therewith content,' 1 Timothy 6:8, the truth that the reward of the faithful is given by God's word and possessed by labour, the inheritance both a gift and a task, 'lay up for yourselves treasures in heaven,' Matthew 6:20, the figure — the believer's portion, modest on earth but secured by God's word, the heavenly inheritance built up by faith and labour, 'he looked for a city which hath foundations, whose builder and maker is God,' Hebrews 11:10, the truth that the servant who gave the people their portions takes a small one himself and builds it, the figure of the modest reward of the faithful, given by God's word and won by labour, the call to be content with God's appointment and to labour in it, the assurance that the faithful servant's portion, though small, is sure and his by God's word, the figure of the modest portion of the faithful servant, given by God's word and possessed by his own labour); Joshua asking and receiving one city, Timnathserah, by the word of the LORD, the modest portion of the leader given by God's appointment (the portion by God's word, 1 Timothy 6:8) — the gift (Timnathserah, by God's word); 'and he built the city, and dwelt therein' (Joshua building up the city and dwelling in it, the inheritance possessed by his own labour, the reward both gift and task, a figure of the heavenly portion built up by faith, the inheritance won by labour, Matthew 6:20; Hebrews 11:10) — the figure (the believer's portion secured by God's word and built up by faith, the providence, the portion, the close of the whole division before the LORD at Shiloh to follow, vv.51) — Calvin, Keil, Ellicott, Gill.

debates.question: What does Joshua's portion of Timnathserah signify?

sides.position: The modest portion of the faithful servant, given by God's word and possessed by his own labour - the great leader asking but one city, given by the word of the LORD and built up by his own hand, content with God's appointment (1 Timothy 6:8), the reward both gift and task possessed by labour (Matthew 6:20), a figure of the heavenly inheritance secured by God's word and built up by faith (Hebrews 11:10)

themes: According to the word of the LORD they gave him the city which he asked, even Timnathserah in mount Ephraim: and he built the city, and dwelt therein
that Joshua received Timnathserah by the word of the LORD and built and dwelt in it (the crux of the modest portion of the faithful servant)
the modesty of the great leader who asked but one city, given by the word of the LORD and built up by his own hand, content with a small inheritance he must labour to make habitable
a figure of the heavenly portion secured by God's word and built up by faith and labour (1 Timothy 6:8; Matthew 6:20; Hebrews 11:10)

schools.summary: The close of the whole division, the inheritances divided by lot in Shiloh before the LORD at the door of the tabernacle: 'these are the inheritances, which Eleazar the priest, and Joshua the son of Nun, and the heads of the fathers of the tribes of the children of Israel, divided for an inheritance by lot in Shiloh before the LORD, at the door of the tabernacle of the congregation. So they made an end of dividing the country' — the division before the LORD — 'these are the inheritances, which Eleazar the priest, and Joshua the son of Nun, and the heads of the fathers... divided for an inheritance by lot in Shiloh before the LORD, at the door of the tabernacle of the congregation. So they made an end of dividing the country': the whole land was divided by lot at Shiloh before the LORD, and the work was finished (the witnesses noting the significance — the inheritance distributed not by man's will but by God's appointment, the lot cast in his presence at the door of the tabernacle, the priest and the leader and the heads of the tribes together dividing the land as God directed, the division done before the LORD, by lot, in the place of his presence, that all might own it as his act and not man's, the lesson — that the inheritance of God's people is wholly of his appointment, given in his presence and by his disposing, 'the lot is cast into the lap; but the whole disposing thereof is of the LORD,' Proverbs 16:33, the truth that God faithfully fulfilled his promise to give Israel the land, the division completed proving his faithfulness, 'there failed not ought of any good thing which the LORD had spoken... all came to pass,' Joshua 21:45, the figure — the inheritance of the saints distributed by God in his presence, each portion appointed, 'in my Father's house are many mansions... I go to prepare a place for you,' John 14:2, the truth that the whole work of dividing was finished, God's promise fully kept, the figure of the inheritance given by God's appointment in his presence, the completion of his faithful promise, the call to receive the portion God appoints as from his hand, the assurance that God finishes the work of giving his people their inheritance, the figure of the inheritance given by God's appointment in his presence, the completion of his faithful promise); the land divided by lot at Shiloh in the presence of the LORD, by the priest, the leader, and the heads of the tribes, the inheritance owned as God's appointment (the division before the LORD, Proverbs 16:33) — the division (by lot before the LORD); 'So they made an end of dividing the country' (the work of dividing finished, God's promise to give the land fully kept, the inheritance of the saints distributed by God, a figure of the heavenly portions prepared, the promise fulfilled, Joshua 21:45; John 14:2) — the figure (the inheritance of the saints distributed by God in his presence, the providence, the completion, the cities of refuge appointed to follow, ch.20) — Calvin, Keil, Ellicott, Gill.

debates.question: What does the division of the land by lot at Shiloh before the LORD establish?

sides.position: The inheritance given by God's appointment in his presence, the completion of his faithful promise - the land distributed not by man's will but by God's disposing, the lot cast before the LORD at the door of the tabernacle and owned as his act (Proverbs 16:33), God faithfully fulfilling his promise to give Israel the land (Joshua 21:45), a figure of the inheritance of the saints distributed by God, each portion appointed (John 14:2)

themes: These are the inheritances, which Eleazar the priest, and Joshua the son of Nun, and the heads of the fathers of the tribes of the children of Israel, divided for an inheritance by lot in Shiloh before the LORD, at the door of the tabernacle of the congregation. So they made an end of dividing the country
the close of the whole division, the inheritances divided by lot in Shiloh before the LORD (the crux of the inheritance given by God's appointment in his presence)
the inheritance distributed not by man's will but by God's appointment, the lot cast in his presence at the door of the tabernacle, owned as his act
God faithfully fulfilling his promise to give Israel the land, a figure of the inheritance of the saints distributed by God in his presence (Proverbs 16:33; Joshua 21:45; John 14:2)
